data.narrative:
Proxima Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Francesco Sciortino, Lucio Milanese, Jorrit Lion, Jonathan Schilling, and Martin Kubie. The company has raised €200M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-sector peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient datapoint concentration. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private companies; filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than three comparable datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. The public market set is similarly constrained at three issuers, limiting cross-check utility. A median derived from the full 11-company set would obscure material differences in technology maturity, funding model, and de-risking trajectory across the sector's heterogeneous participant base.

This analysis underscores a structural challenge in valuing Proxima Fusion: the frontier nuclear sector remains sufficiently nascent and dispersed that traditional peer-median approaches lack statistical power. Forward valuation work would benefit from staged re-assessment as either the public comp set expands or private-round data becomes available from nearer-stage peers. Capital efficiency metrics and technology-stage milestones may prove more informative than raw valuation multiples at this juncture.
